steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank transitions between two sentences: (1) Najas marina can thrive in nickel-polluted soil, and (2) it is a hyperaccumulator that absorbs and sequesters nickel. The second sentence adds a further, elaborating fact about the same subject — it does not reverse, result from, or contradict the first.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Consequently,': marks a result or effect. But being a hyperaccumulator is not a consequence of thriving in nickel soil — it is the mechanism that explains the thriving. Cause-effect is backwards. ✗
  • Choice B — 'By contrast,': signals opposition or difference. The two sentences describe the same plant in the same context; there is no contrast. ✗
  • Choice C — 'What's more,': adds an elaborating or intensifying point to what was just said. Sentence 2 explains how Najas marina manages nickel — additional information that builds on sentence 1 without contradicting it. ✓
  • Choice D — 'Nevertheless,': signals concession or contrast despite an obstacle. Nothing in sentence 2 concedes or pushes back against sentence 1. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
